title: cerastium alpinum subsp. suffruticosum
intro: Cerastium alpinum subsp. suffruticosum, commonly known as Alpine Chickweed, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Alpine Chickweed include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Alpine Chickweed can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote skin health and well-being.
